skill.name: azure-upgrade
skill.description: Assess and upgrade Azure workloads between plans, tiers, or SKUs within Azure. Generates assessment reports and automates upgrade steps. WHEN: upgrade Consumption to Flex Consumption, upgrade Azure Functions plan, migrate hosting plan, upgrade Functions SKU, move to Flex Consumption, upgrade Azure service tier, change hosting plan, upgrade function app plan, migrate App Service to Container Apps.
skill.summary: Official Microsoft skill for assessing and upgrading Azure workloads between plans, tiers, or SKUs with automated migration scripts.
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: This is an official Microsoft skill providing Azure upgrade documentation. The static scanner detected patterns in markdown code examples - these are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill contains documentation with example Azure CLI commands, not executable code. All detected external_commands, network, and filesystem patterns are in documentation context. No actual code execution occurs - the skill provides guidance to Claude for helping users perform legitimate Azure upgrades.

critical_findings.title: False Positive: Code Execution + Network + Credential Access Heuristic
critical_findings.description: The static scanner flagged a CRITICAL heuristic for 'suspicious behavior patterns' (code execution + network + credential access). This is a FALSE POSITIVE - the skill is legitimate Microsoft documentation that describes Azure CLI commands for performing upgrades. The 'code execution' is bash/PowerShell examples in markdown documentation, 'network' is Azure API calls made by user-approved Azure CLI operations, and there is no credential exfiltration - the skill uses official Azure MCP tools for authenticated operations.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: This is an official Microsoft skill from the azure-skills repository. The detected patterns are documentation code blocks, not actual code execution. Azure operations are performed through official MCP tools with user authentication.

high_findings.title: False Positive: PowerShell Invocation Detection
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'PowerShell invocation' at multiple locations in automation.md and consumption-to-flex.md. These are FALSE POSITIVES - the findings are detecting PowerShell syntax in markdown documentation code blocks, not actual PowerShell execution. The skill provides example commands for users to run in their own Azure Cloud Shell.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are documentation code blocks in markdown files - the skill is a documentation reference, not executable code. Users run these commands themselves in their Azure environment.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at various locations in global-rules.md, automation.md, consumption-to-flex.md, workflow-details.md, and SKILL.md. These are FALSE POSITIVES - the scanner is likely triggered by text mentioning hash functions or authentication methods in documentation context. The skill promotes identity-based authentication (managed identity, DefaultAzureCredential) as documented in global-rules.md.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly advocates for managed identity over connection strings. These findings are triggered by documentation text mentioning authentication concepts, not actual weak cryptography implementation.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Shell Command Detection in Documentation
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 207 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' and 'Shell command substitution' findings across multiple files. These are FALSE POSITIVES - the findings are triggered by code blocks (backtick fences) in markdown documentation describing Azure CLI commands. The skill does not execute any commands - it provides documentation and guidance.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All 207 findings are in markdown documentation files. The skill uses markdown code fences to display example Azure CLI commands. No actual shell execution occurs - these are documentation examples for users to follow.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Network Hardcoded URL Detection
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Hardcoded URL' findings at various locations. These are FALSE POSITIVES - the URLs point to official Microsoft Learn documentation (learn.microsoft.com), which is expected for an official Microsoft skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URLs are legitimate Microsoft Learn documentation links. This is expected and appropriate for an official Microsoft skill.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Filesystem Detection
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Standard device file access' at locations in automation.md. These are FALSE POSITIVES - the findings are triggered by documentation text about file paths and logs, not actual filesystem operations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are documentation references to log files and output paths in example commands. No actual filesystem operations occur - the skill is documentation-only.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Detection
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'System reconnaissance' findings at various locations. These are FALSE POSITIVES - the skill documents how to assess Azure resources (list apps, check compatibility) which is a legitimate upgrade assessment workflow, not system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These findings are triggered by documentation describing Azure resource queries (list apps, check settings). This is standard Azure administration for upgrade assessment, not malicious reconnaissance.

content.user_title: Upgrade Azure Functions and App Service Plans
content.value_statement: This official Microsoft skill automates the assessment and upgrade of Azure workloads between plans, tiers, and SKUs. It generates detailed assessment reports and provides step-by-step automation scripts for migrating from Consumption to Flex Consumption plans and other upgrade scenarios.
actual_capabilities: Assess Azure Functions and App Service workloads for upgrade readiness
Generate detailed assessment reports with compatibility checks
Automate upgrade from Consumption plan to Flex Consumption plan
Provide Azure CLI and PowerShell scripts for plan migrations
Validate upgraded applications with health checks
Guide users through multi-phase upgrade workflows with safety checks
limitations: Only supports Azure-native upgrades within the same cloud provider
Does not perform cross-cloud migrations (use azure-cloud-migrate skill)
Does not support in-process .NET functions (must migrate to isolated first)
Does not support deployment slots or TLS certificates in Flex Consumption

use_cases.title: Upgrade Functions to Flex Consumption
use_cases.description: Migrate an Azure Functions app from Consumption plan to Flex Consumption plan for better performance and features
use_cases.target_user: Azure developers and DevOps engineers modernizing their serverless workloads

use_cases.title: Assess Upgrade Readiness
use_cases.description: Evaluate whether an existing Azure workload is ready for a plan or SKU upgrade before making changes
use_cases.target_user: Cloud architects and IT teams planning infrastructure upgrades

use_cases.title: Automate Migration Steps
use_cases.description: Execute automated scripts to perform the actual upgrade with validation and rollback guidance
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers and administrators performing production upgrades

prompt_templates.title: Basic Upgrade Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Upgrade my Azure Functions app named 'myfunctionapp' from Consumption plan to Flex Consumption plan in resource group 'myrg'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple upgrade request with all parameters provided

prompt_templates.title: Assessment Before Upgrade
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess whether my function app 'myapp' in resource group 'rg-eastus' is ready to upgrade to Flex Consumption. Check region compatibility, runtime support, and any blockers.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Step Upgrade
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me upgrade my Azure Functions app from Consumption to Flex Consumption. First assess readiness, then perform the upgrade, validate the app works, and clean up the old resources.
prompt_templates.scenario: Full upgrade workflow with assessment, execution, validation, and cleanup

prompt_templates.title: Upgrade with Custom Settings
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to upgrade my Python function app to Flex Consumption in WestUS2 region. The app uses Python 3.11. Generate the upgrade script and validate the new endpoint responds correctly.
prompt_templates.scenario: Upgrade with specific runtime and region requirements

output_examples.input: Upgrade my function app from Consumption to Flex Consumption
output: Phase 1: Assessment - Checking region compatibility, runtime stack (Python 3.11), and pre-requisites
Phase 2: Pre-migration - Collecting app settings, identity configuration, and deployment details
Phase 3: Upgrade - Creating new Flex Consumption app and migrating settings
Phase 4: Validation - Testing endpoint availability and monitoring configuration
Upgrade complete! The new Flex Consumption app is running at https://myapp.azurewebsites.net

output_examples.input: Is my function app ready for Flex Consumption?
output: - Region: EastUS (✓ Supported for Flex Consumption)

best_practices: Always run assessment phase before performing any upgrade to identify blockers
Keep the original app running until the upgraded app is fully validated
Use managed identity connections instead of connection strings for improved security
Confirm target configuration with user before executing destructive operations
anti_patterns: Do not skip the assessment phase - always check compatibility first
Do not delete the original app without explicit user confirmation
Do not upgrade production apps without testing in a staging environment first
Do not assume all regions support all features - verify region compatibility

faq.question: What Azure services does this skill support upgrading?
faq.answer: Currently supports Azure Functions upgrades including Consumption to Flex Consumption plan migrations. Additional service support may be added in future updates.

faq.question: Does this skill delete my original app during upgrade?
faq.answer: No. The skill keeps the original app running until the upgraded app is validated. You must explicitly confirm before any deletion occurs.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for cross-cloud migrations?
faq.answer: No. This skill is for Azure-to-Azure upgrades within the same cloud. For cross-cloud migrations, use the azure-cloud-migrate skill instead.

faq.question: What happens if the upgrade fails?
faq.answer: The skill provides rollback guidance and keeps your original app running. You can restart the original app while issues are resolved.

faq.question: Does this skill work with all programming languages?
faq.answer: Supports .NET isolated, Node.js, Java, Python, PowerShell, and custom handlers. Does not support in-process .NET - must migrate to isolated first.

faq.question: How does this skill authenticate with Azure?
faq.answer: Uses official Azure MCP tools with user authentication. The skill recommends managed identity over connection strings for better security.
